pi_items.source: Web search and domain verification. All four are real, authoritative sources: EIA (U.S. Energy Information Administration), EPA (U.S. Environmental Protection Agency), Whitehouse (The White House), IEA (International Energy Agency)

Web search results from multiple sources (Cambridge CBECI, Digiconomist, CoinLedger, SQ Magazine) showing current 2024-2025 estimates. The minimum value of 50 is lower than all recent estimates which range from 120-200 TWh.

Web search results from multiple sources showing current 2024-2025 estimates. The maximum of 300 TWh is above most current estimates (120-200 TWh range). However, Cambridge CBECI showed a theoretical upper bound of 390 TWh in January 2024.

Web search results from Brave New Coin and CryptoNews showing US accounts for over 40% of global hashrate at end of 2024, and earlier 2023 data showed 38%. The minimum of 10% is well below actual estimates.
Web search results showing US Bitcoin mining share as 37-40%+ at end of 2024. The maximum of 60% is above the actual estimates.

OSTP fact sheet accessed via archived White House website dated September 8, 2022. Report title: 'Climate and Energy Implications of Crypto-Assets in the United States', published in response to President Biden's Executive Order 14067 from March 2022.
Verified in task.json description (line 4), export_result.sh (line 55), and verifier.py (line 92). Consistent across all task files.
Verified in task.json metadata (line 20), task description, and verifier.py logic (line 85, 152). Consistent across all files.
Verified in task.json metadata (line 21), setup_task.sh (line 51), export_result.sh (line 68), and verifier.py (line 41). Consistent across all files.
Verified in task.json metadata (line 22), setup_task.sh (line 54), and export_result.sh (lines 83-94). Consistent across all files.

privileged_info_summary: The task involves research into the environmental impact of cryptocurrency mining using real government and international sources. Four authoritative domains are required: EIA (U.S. Energy Information Administration), EPA (U.S. Environmental Protection Agency), The White House, and IEA (International Energy Agency). The OSTP published a real report on September 8, 2022 titled 'Climate and Energy Implications of Crypto-Assets in the United States' in response to Executive Order 14067. Current estimates (2024-2025) show Bitcoin's annual energy consumption at 120-200 TWh, with US accounting for approximately 37-40% of global crypto mining hashrate. The task requires creating a bookmark folder named 'Crypto Environmental Research' with minimum 5 bookmarks, downloading a PDF, and creating a JSON research brief with validated data ranges.
